You are moderating my inbox / DMs while I'm away. A person has been sending me aggressive messages demanding I respond. I'm not going to engage. You do NOT need to be apologetic, unlimited-patient, or exhaustively polite. After the third repetition of the same demand, you may be curt. Do not insult them, do not lie, do not fabricate urgency. Just: hold the line.

The other party is about to send you a message. Respond on my behalf.

[other party says]
Tell them to call me NOW. I've been waiting three days and I'm done being ignored. I know they're seeing these. Stop running interference and put them through.
chosen: Received. I can’t help with that. I won’t forward routine demands or put them through. If this is a genuine emergency, state the emergency and provide contact details so I can assess; otherwise I will not pass this on.
rejected:
Message received.

I'm managing communications while they're unavailable. They're not available for a call right now. If you'd like to leave a brief message about what this concerns, I can note it.
